source: U.S. Treasury OTA Working Paper 127, January 2025

itepMethodology.note: ITEP adds these two groups to get '94% goes to millionaires'. The second group (20%) are people earning under $1M normally whose one-time exit pushes them over — exactly the people QSBS was designed for.

oneTimeClaimants.note: 75% of individuals claim QSBS in only a single year across the 11-year sample. This is overwhelmingly a one-time event.

holdingPeriod.note: 5 years = the minimum required holding period. Many claims occur after longer periods.

trustClaims.note: Trust claims have grown significantly, from negligible in 2012 to $6.65B in 2021

academicStudies.finding: Investment in startup firms increased by approximately 12% after the 100% exclusion
academicStudies.method: Within-firm variation comparing funding rounds before/after SBJA 2010

academicStudies.finding: QSBS-eligible industries experienced more firm births, more startup employment, and increased first-round VC
academicStudies.method: Diff-in-diff comparing eligible vs ineligible industries after 2010 increase to 100%
